previous_statement: First insertion (FI): ||P(Px‚ąėb)-P(x‚ąėb)|| <= C sqrt(eta) ||x|| ||b|| for all x in V and b in A=Im P (def near-fixed-algebra). PROOF: Set n:=x-Px. Then Pn=Px-P^2 x=Px-Px=0 (P^2=P, P real-linear, GT-Pprops), so n in Ker P; and ||n||=||x-Px|| <= ||1-P|| ||x|| <= (1+||P||)||x|| <= C||x|| (GT-Pprops ||P||<=1+C eta). By P real-linear, P(x‚ąėb)-P(Px‚ąėb)=P((x-Px)‚ąėb)=P(n‚ąėb). Fix any state rho on B(H), omega=rho‚ąėPhi. Then |rho(P(n‚ąėb))| <= |rho(Phi(n‚ąėb))| + |rho((P-Phi)(n‚ąėb))|. The second term: ||(P-Phi)(n‚ąėb)|| <= ||P-Phi|| ||n‚ąėb|| = delta ||n‚ąėb|| <= delta ||n|| ||b|| (delta=||P-Phi||<=C eta GT-Pprops; ||n‚ąėb|| <= ||n|| ||b|| by the JB product bound GT-jb-axiom-sq context / submultiplicativity of ‚ąė); since rho is a state, |rho((P-Phi)(n‚ąėb))| <= ||(P-Phi)(n‚ąėb)|| <= C eta ||n|| ||b||. The first term equals |omega(n‚ąėb)| (omega=rho‚ąėPhi). Now n in Ker P and b in Im P (b in A=Im P), so by almost-orthogonality (node 1.3) with u=b: |omega(n‚ąėb)|=|omega(b‚ąėn)| <= C sqrt(eta) ||b|| ||n||. Combining: |rho(P(n‚ąėb))| <= C sqrt(eta)||n||||b|| + C eta||n||||b|| <= C sqrt(eta)||n||||b|| <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b|| (using ||n||<=C||x||, eta<=sqrt(eta) for eta<1). Since P(n‚ąėb) is self-adjoint (P maps V to V, n‚ąėb in V), take the supremum over all states rho on V=B(H)_sa: ||P(n‚ąėb)||=sup_{rho in S(V)}|rho(P(n‚ąėb))| (GT-sup-states, HOS 1.2.5(ii)) <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b||. Hence ||P(Px‚ąėb)-P(x‚ąėb)||=||P(n‚ąėb)|| <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b||. Uses node 1.3, GT-Pprops, GT-sup-states, GT-jb-axiom-sq, def near-fixed-algebra, def jordan-product.
new_statement: First insertion (FI): ||P(Px‚ąėb)-P(x‚ąėb)|| <= C sqrt(eta) ||x|| ||b|| for all x in V and b in A=Im P (def near-fixed-algebra). PROOF: Set n:=x-Px. Then Pn=Px-P^2 x=Px-Px=0 (P^2=P, P real-linear, GT-Pprops), so n in Ker P; and ||n||=||x-Px|| <= ||x||+||Px|| <= ||x||+||P|| ||x|| <= (2+C eta)||x|| <= C||x|| (GT-Pprops ||P||<=1+C eta; triangle ineq in V). By P real-linear (GT-Pprops), P(x‚ąėb)-P(Px‚ąėb)=P((x-Px)‚ąėb)=P(n‚ąėb). Fix any state rho on B(H), omega=rho‚ąėPhi. Then |rho(P(n‚ąėb))| <= |rho(Phi(n‚ąėb))| + |rho((P-Phi)(n‚ąėb))|. SECOND term: ||(P-Phi)(n‚ąėb)|| <= ||P-Phi|| ||n‚ąėb|| = delta ||n‚ąėb|| <= delta ||n|| ||b|| (delta=||P-Phi||<=C eta GT-Pprops; ||n‚ąėb|| <= ||n|| ||b|| by the JB-product norm axiom GT-jb-prod-submult, V=B(H)_sa a JB algebra GT-bhsa-jc/GT-jb-orderunit-3310); since rho is a state on B(H), |rho(y)| <= ||y|| for self-adjoint y (analogue of node 1.1's bound, GT-jb-orderunit-3310), so |rho((P-Phi)(n‚ąėb))| <= C eta ||n|| ||b||. FIRST term equals |omega(n‚ąėb)| (omega=rho‚ąėPhi). Now n in Ker P and b in Im P (b in A=Im P), so by almost-orthogonality (node 1.3) with u=b: |omega(n‚ąėb)|=|omega(b‚ąėn)| <= C sqrt(eta) ||b|| ||n|| (n‚ąėb=b‚ąėn, def jordan-product symmetric). Combining: |rho(P(n‚ąėb))| <= C sqrt(eta)||n||||b|| + C eta||n||||b|| <= C sqrt(eta)||n||||b|| <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b|| (||n||<=C||x||; eta<=sqrt(eta) for eta<1). Since P(n‚ąėb) is self-adjoint (P:V->V, n‚ąėb in V), take the supremum over all states rho on V=B(H)_sa: ||P(n‚ąėb)||=sup_{rho in S(V)}|rho(P(n‚ąėb))| (GT-sup-states, HOS 1.2.5(ii)) <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b||. Hence ||P(Px‚ąėb)-P(x‚ąėb)||=||P(n‚ąėb)|| <= C sqrt(eta)||x||||b||. Uses node 1.3, GT-Pprops, GT-sup-states, GT-jb-prod-submult, GT-bhsa-jc, GT-jb-orderunit-3310, def near-fixed-algebra, def jordan-product.
